title: Crispy Air-Fry Slovakian Yukon Gold Potato Fritters
description: A sick-day dish featuring crispy air-fry Yukon gold potatoes, perfect for a comforting Slovakian treat. These sour, shaggy fritters are egg-free-friendly and sure to satisfy your cravings.

ingredients.name: Chopped Fresh Dill
ingredients.notes: Fresh dill adds a bright flavor to these fritters.

ingredients.name: White Vinegar
ingredients.notes: White wine vinegar or apple cider vinegar can be substituted.

ingredients.name: Granulated Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances the acidity from the vinegar.

ingredients.name: Cold Water
ingredients.notes: Ice-cold water helps create a crispy exterior.

ingredients.name: Breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: Optional, but recommended for added crunch.

instructions: Mash the Yukon Gold Potatoes in a large bowl with a fork until they are coarsely mashed.
Add the all-purpose flour, rye flour, salt, black pepper, and sugar to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Stir in the melted butter, chopped dill, sour cream, white vinegar, and cold water until the mixture is sticky and holds together.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into 8-10 patties, about 1/2 inch thick.
Place the patties in the air fryer basket in a single layer, leaving about 1 inch of space between each fritter.
Air-fry the fritters at 375°F (190°C) for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
Flip the fritters halfway through cooking to ensure even browning.
Serve the fritters hot with a dollop of sour cream or a sprinkle of chopped fresh dill, if desired.
Optional: Prepare a bowl of breadcrumbs mixed with a pinch of salt and a few grates of black pepper for dusting the fritters before serving.
